channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: The week that Trump pushed the global economy to the brink with tariffs
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The stock market was soaring and the sun was shining when President Donald Trump stepped out of the Oval Office on Wednesday afternoon. Less than two hours earlier, he had retreated from his plans to increase tariffs on many U.S.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN and SEUNG MIN KIM, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: DOJ asks to dismiss Virginia case against Salvadoran accused MS-13 leader set to be deported
item.description: The DOJ has moved to dismiss its case against Salvadoran national Henrry Josue Villatoro Santos, who is accused of being an MS-13 leader while residing in Virginia.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New report pushes for permanent pedestrian-only Bourbon Street to boost Mardi Gras safety after terror attack
item.description: A new report urges changes to make Bourbon Street safer after a deadly New Year’s Day attack, including turning the New Orleans strip into a pedestrian zone.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Americans convicted in Congo of a botched coup attempt now face US charges
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (AP) — Three Americans repatriated to the United States from Congo this week have been charged with participating in an elaborate coup attempt aimed at overthrowing the African nation’s government last year, the U.S. Justice Department said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH SCHOENBAUM and ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Trump orders probe into 'Anonymous' New York Times editorial writer
item.description: President Trump on Wednesday signed a pair of memorandums directing the Department of Justice to investigate two individuals who served in his first administration and pushed back against Trump.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 1,700 pounds of butter recalled over bacteria contamination
item.description: Nearly a ton of salted butter is being recalled because, according to the U.S. Food and Drug Administration, the product is "contaminated with elevated levels of coliform," a bacteria found in animals' digestive tracts and waste.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: She survived being shot in the head as a teen. Now she's sharing her final message
item.description: Deserae Turner — who survived being shot in the head in 2017 and has undergone several medical procedures since — is now in hospice after recent health complications.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Pennsylvania judge is convicted of shooting and wounding her ex-boyfriend as he slept
item.description: HARRISBURG, Pa. (AP) — A magistrate judge in Pennsylvania accused of shooting and wounding her ex-boyfriend in the head as he slept was convicted Wednesday of attempted homicide and aggravated assault charges.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Sam's Club plans to open 15 new locations a year, CEO says
item.description: Sam's Club CEO Chris Nicholas says the company plans to open 15 new locations a year "for the foreseeable future.
item.dc:creator: Kyler Swaim, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US Postal Service seeks to hike cost of a first-class stamp
item.description: If approved, the increase for a “forever” stamp and similar increases for postcards, metered letters and international mail would take effect July 13.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: LeBron James becomes first pro male athlete to have likeness depicted in Ken doll
item.description: LeBron James has become the first professional male athlete to have his likeness depicted in a Ken doll.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Vice president of Bikers Against Child Abuse chapter arrested for sex crimes against children
item.description: New York State Police said the vice president of Buffalo's Bikers Against Child Abuse chapter has been arrested for sex crimes against children.
item.dc:creator: Kayleigh Hunter-Gasperini, WFLA, NBC

item.title: US Postal Service seeks to hike cost of a first-class stamp to 78 cents
item.description: The U.S. Postal Service is seeking a rate increase this summer that includes hiking the cost of a first-class stamp from 73 cents to 78 cents.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Johnson aiming for budget vote Wednesday, but suggests support is not yet there
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) said Wednesday that he’s still aiming to stage a vote later in the evening on a Senate-passed budget bill to guide President Trump’s domestic agenda, but suggested the support is not yet there to pass it and the final vote will come later than 5:30 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump replaces Patel with Army secretary as ATF director
item.description: FBI Director Kash Patel has been removed as the acting director of the Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco, Firearms and Explosives.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: She survived being shot in the head as teen. Now she's sharing her final message
item.description: Deserae Turner — who survived being shot in the head in 2017 and has undergone several medical procedures since — is now in hospice after recent health complications.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump tariffs, including massive levies on China, go into effect
item.description: President Donald Trump's latest round of sweeping reciprocal tariffs went into effect early Wednesday, including a 104 percent import tax on China.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KRON4

item.title: The Latest: Treasury secretary says Trump will keep 10% import tariff, raise import tax on China
item.description: President Donald Trump has shared on Truth Social that he has “authorized a 90 day PAUSE, and a substantially lowered Reciprocal Tariff during this period, of 10%, also effective immediately.”.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: 96-year-old resident at southern Utah care home dies after being found in pool
item.description: A resident of a senior living facility in southern Utah has died after being found in the community's pool on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, ABC 4

item.title: Trump signs executive orders to boost coal, a reliable but polluting energy source
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump on Tuesday signed a series of executive orders aimed at boosting the struggling coal industry, a reliable but polluting energy source that’s long been in decline.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW DALY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: UVU Ballroom Dance Team wins first place at World Culture Dance Competition
item.description: The Utah Valley University Ballroom Dance Company Tour Team took home the first place award at the World Culture Dance Competition this last weekend.
item.dc:creator: Kayla Baggerly, ABC 4

item.title: Surgery nightmare: Hospital admits mistake after failed cancer surgery attempt
item.description: An investigation is underway at MountainView Hospital in Las Vegas after a cancer patient woke up to startling news in the operating room.
item.dc:creator: Vanessa Murphy, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Human remains found in 1973 identified as missing Kansas teen
item.description: Human remains found near Garnett, Kansas, in 1973 have been positively identified more than 50 years later.
item.dc:creator: Brian Dulle, WFLA, NBC

item.title: A Florida man is executed for killing a Miami Herald employee who was abducted on her lunch break
item.description: STARKE, Fla. (AP) — A Florida man convicted of killing a Miami Herald employee who was abducted on her lunch break was executed Tuesday evening. Michael Tanzi was pronounced dead at 6:12 p.m.
item.dc:creator: CURT ANDERSON, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Human remains found in 1973 identified as missing Kansas teen
item.description: Human remains found near Garnett, Kansas, in 1973 have been positively identified more than 50 years later.
item.dc:creator: Brian Dulle, KRON4

item.title: Waiver allows Trump administration to bypass environmental rules to build more border walls
item.description: SAN DIEGO (AP) — A waiver issued Tuesday by the U.S. Department of Homeland Security allows the federal government to bypass environmental regulations and begin construction to add more barriers along the U.S-Mexico border in Southern California even as illegal crossings have plummeted.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Judge rejects new trials for 2 convicted of human smuggling in death of family of 4 from India
item.description: MINNEAPOLIS (AP) — A federal judge on Tuesday rejected requests for new trials for two men convicted on human smuggling charges in the deaths of four members of a family from India who froze to death while trying to cross the Canadian border into Minnesota during a blizzard in 2022. U.S.
item.dc:creator: STEVE KARNOWSKI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: National Weather Service no longer translating products for non-English speakers
item.description: The National Weather Service is no longer providing language translations of its products, a change that experts say could put non-English speakers at risk of missing potentially life-saving warnings about extreme weather.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Veterinarian goes missing after video showed him kicking horse
item.description: A Las Vegas veterinarian is missing following a viral video that shows him kicking a horse in the head. On Thursday, Dr. Shawn Frehner was paid to administer anesthetic shots for some horses at a Nevada property.
item.dc:creator: James Schaeffer, KTLA 5

item.title: More Americans call taxes unfair: Poll
item.description: More Americans say that the amount they will pay in federal income taxes this year is "unfair" than those who say their amount is reasonable, according to a new poll, and most say that corporations and wealthy people are not paying enough.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, KRON4

item.title: Supreme Court clears way for Trump admin to fire some probationary workers
item.description: The Supreme Court sided with the Trump administration Tuesday by enabling officials to fire thousands of federal workers in their probationary period, saying the government employee unions that sued don’t have legal standing.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, ABC 4

item.title: California man arrested near Justice Kavanaugh's home with a gun pleads guilty to attempted murder
item.description: GREENBELT, Md. (AP) — A California man pleaded guilty on Tuesday to trying to kill U.S. Supreme Court Justice Brett Kavanaugh at his home in a suburb of Washington, D.C., nearly three years ago. Nicholas John Roske was arrested near Kavanaugh’s home in Chevy Chase, Maryland, in June 2022.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: ICE agent impersonations spike in wake of immigration crackdown
item.description: Impersonatorsposing as immigration agents are spreading fear among migrants already uncertain about their future in the country.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Former Sen. Joe Manchin is working on a memoir. 'Dead Center' is scheduled for September
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Former Sen. Joe Manchin has a memoir coming out in September, what the self-styled maverick from West Virginia calls his “declaration of independence from the extremes on both sides.” St.
item.dc:creator: HILLEL ITALIE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Supreme Court allows Trump to deport Venezuelans under wartime law, but only after judges' review
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court on Monday allowed the Trump administration to use an 18th century wartime law to deport Venezuelan migrants, but said they must get a court hearing before they are taken from the United States.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Scientists genetically engineer wolves with white hair and muscular jaws like the extinct dire wolf
item.description: Three genetically engineered wolves that may resemble extinct dire wolves are trotting, sleeping and howling in an undisclosed secure location in the U.S., according to the company that aims to bring back lost species.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTINA LARSON, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump threatens additional 50 percent tariff on China
item.description: The White House had already imposed a 20 percent tariff on Chinese imports over fentanyl concerns, meaning China could face an effective tariff rate as high as 70 percent.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5
